task:
[Read from: /home/spencer/Repositories/predictability-aware-training/plan.md, /home/spencer/Repositories/predictability-aware-training/progress.md]

RED-TEAM ROUND 3 — systems claims and the planned real-engine demo at /home/spencer/Repositories/predictability-aware-training. Read RESULTS.md Exp 5/8 (cache sim, TTFT, sensitivity), cache_sim.py, and RESEARCH.md's Colibri section. The project plans to wire a trained predictor into Colibri's PILOT path (github.com/JustVugg/colibri, cloned at /tmp/pi-github-repos/JustVugg/colibri if present, else consider its README architecture: c/olmoe.c backend, PILOT=1 router-lookahead thread, per-layer LRU, async I/O pool, batch-union). Attack: (1) Are the sim's final corrected numbers (+3.2% tok/s joint-vs-base, -31% waste, +21-24% prediction-general) internally consistent and correctly computed? Re-verify key rows by rerunning cache_sim.py yourself (CPU). (2) The sim's policy (one fetch per idle window, demand-priority FIFO) vs Colibri's real PILOT (router-lookahead thread prefetching next layer's experts into an async pool) — will the demo likely show LARGER or SMALLER effects than the sim? Sign and magnitude reasoning. (3) The demo plan: OLMoE-1B-7B via c/olmoe.c + trained linear predictor heads (2048x64 per layer-horizon) exported and used for prefetch instead of/in addition to PILOT's router lookahead. What are the top 3 technical risks (weight conversion, hidden-state access inside the C engine, comparator fairness)? Propose the cheapest experimental design that would produce ONE defensible end-to-end number. (4) What could make the demo look good but be misleading (warm page cache, batch-union effects, measurement windows)? (5) Is OLMoE-on-Colibri even the right demo given Tier C showed the OLMoE backbone didn't change — should the demo instead use the Tier A toy model with a purpose-built small engine? READ-ONLY. Ranked findings + concrete design recommendation.

criteria.must: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable
Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

criteriaSatisfied.evidence: Ranked findings cite cache_sim.py:79-93,113-124,152,167 and RESULTS.md Exp 5/8/10 with severities (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW); all Exp 5/8 numbers re-verified by rerunning cache_sim.py

commandsRun.summary: All 18 rows match RESULTS.md Exp 5 exactly (demand 0.876, base-h1 1.051, joint-h1 1.085, oracle 1.161, waste 34.6/24.0)
TTFT 2398/2156/2133/2110ms and p50 1142/947/920/862ms match Exp 8 exactly
Fits-in-window guard blocks all prefetch at 3.5GB/s; every policy equals demand (0.670) as claimed in Exp 8
Sensitivity corner: joint-vs-base +2.9%, joint-vs-demand +21.4%, inside claimed +2.9-3.2% / +21-24% bands

validationOutput: +3.2% joint-vs-base (1.085/1.051), -30.6% waste (24.0/34.6), 30.9% headroom capture (0.034/0.110), +23.9% joint-vs-demand all re-derived from raw sim output and consistent with RESULTS.md
residualRisks: Sim injects 16-expert top-2 accuracies into a 256-expert top-8 geometry; absolute tok/s magnitudes unvalidated at real scale (cache_sim.py:152)
OLMoE demo cannot show the thesis: Exp 10 isolation test failed (posthoc-on-joint == posthoc-on-base, 0.800 vs 0.799)
Colibri source unavailable locally (/tmp/pi-github-repos/JustVugg empty); PILOT analysis based on README summary in RESEARCH.md
reviewFindings: no blockers in the corrected sim numbers; all Exp 5/8 rows reproduce exactly
HIGH: cache_sim.py:152 - synthetic scenario transplants Tier-A 16-expert accuracies (0.826/0.888) into 256-expert Colibri geometry; absolute magnitudes optimistic vs Colibri's reported 71.6%

HIGH: RESULTS.md Exp 10 - OLMoE backbone did not become more predictable under LoRA FT, so the planned Colibri demo can only show predictor-vs-PILOT, not the training-induced effect; recommend Tier A + purpose-built small engine instead

MEDIUM: demo hazards on this box - 102GB free RAM vs ~4GB model means warm page cache will fake disk behavior without O_DIRECT/drop_caches; batch-union and in-distribution prompts further bias toward misleading-good results
MEDIUM: top demo risks = hidden-state access inside c/olmoe.c, comparator fairness vs free PILOT router-lookahead, HF-to-C weight conversion parity
childReport.manualNotes: Sim is internally consistent and honestly constructed (bandwidth-conserving, demand-priority, conservative one-fetch-per-idle-window). The +3.2% joint-vs-base delta is trustworthy as a toy-geometry prediction; the recommended validation is a minimal O_DIRECT engine on the Tier A checkpoints measuring tok/s(joint backbone)/tok/s(base backbone) under identical posthoc predictors, cold cache, B=1 - the cheapest design yielding one defensible end-to-end number.
